message:
refactor(abi): self-host default_efun + HTM clock on raw syscalls

The efun and htm_fast_path code paths were the last two callers in
crates/frankenlibc-abi/src that still reached into std::Instant /
libc::write / libc::exit instead of going through the project's own
raw_syscall layer. That is the wrong direction for a "Rust libc that
also replaces glibc" — once frankenlibc preloads itself in a process,
those host-libc calls become re-entrant calls into the very symbols
we have just replaced.

efun_abi.rs (default_efun)
  - Drop libc::write/libc::exit. Use raw_syscall::sys_write to push the
    error message and trailing newline directly to fd 2, then dispatch
    process termination through stdlib_abi::exit (which itself bottoms
    out in raw exit syscalls).
  - Mark default_efun as `-> !` to encode the actual contract that this
    path never returns and to let the compiler statically prove the
    fallthrough is unreachable.

htm_fast_path.rs (now_ms)
  - Replace the `OnceLock<Instant>` epoch + `Instant::elapsed().as_millis()`
    pair with a CLOCK_MONOTONIC read via raw_syscall::sys_clock_gettime
    into a stack-local libc::timespec, plus an AtomicU64 nanosecond
    epoch installed once via compare_exchange.
  - Saturating math everywhere so a kernel timespec returning unexpected
    values cannot panic.
  - Removes the std::time::Instant import and the std::sync::OnceLock
    import; the only synchronization left is the existing AtomicU64s
    used by the HTM sample window machinery.

Both changes are pure refactors at the public ABI level — no symbol
signatures change and no behavior changes for callers, but the failure
mode under self-preload is no longer "infinite recursion through our
own libc::write replacement."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
